title: clausena dentata var. longipes
intro: Clausena dentata var. longipes, also known as the Longipedes Clausena or Longipedes Toothpick Tree, is a tropical plant species native to Southeast Asia, belonging to the Rutaceae family. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating fever, digestive issues, and respiratory problems, due to its antipyretic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Clausena dentata var. longipes include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate pain, and improve digestion, as well as its potential to act as an antiviral and antibacterial agent. Clausena dentata var. longipes is commonly used in herbal preparations, such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be administered orally to harness its medicinal properties.
